command_examples: Dump revelant information on compromised targets without AV detection. Version: 2.0.0
usage: dpp collect [-h] [--keep-collecting seconds] [--threads Number of threads] [--no-config] [-t TARGET [TARGET ...]] [-d domain.local]

documentation.readme: DonPAPI automates secrets dump remotely on multiple Windows computers, with defense evasion in mind.
usage_sections: Installation: This tool should be install with pipx or in a dedicated virtual environment or (with latest commits) or (to dev)

arguments.name: --dc-ip
arguments.description: IP Address of the domain controller. If omitted it will use the domain part (FQDN) specified in the target parameter

arguments.name: --fetch-pvk
arguments.description: Will automatically use domain backup key from database, and if not already dumped, will dump it on a domain controller

arguments.name: -h
arguments.description: show this help message and exit

arguments.name: --help
arguments.description: show this help message and exit

arguments.name: -k
arguments.description: Use Kerberos authentication. Grabs credentials from ccache file (KRB5CCNAME) based on target parameters. If valid

arguments.name: --laps
arguments.description: use LAPS to request local admin password. The laps parameter value is the local admin account use to connect

arguments.name: --no-config
arguments.description: Do not load donpapi config file (~/.donpapi/donpapi.conf)

arguments.name: --no-remoteops
arguments.description: Disable Remote Ops operations (basically no Remote Registry operations, no DPAPI System Credentials)

arguments.name: -nr
arguments.description: Disable Remote Ops operations (basically no Remote Registry operations, no DPAPI System Credentials)

arguments.name: --ntfile
arguments.description: File containing username:nthash that will be used eventually to decrypt masterkeys

arguments.name: --pvkfile
arguments.description: Pvk file with domain backup key

arguments.name: --pwdfile
arguments.description: File containing username:password that will be used eventually to decrypt masterkeys

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the frameworks category. Fits when the engagement needs: DonPAPI automates secrets dump remotely on multiple Windows computers, with defense evasion in mind.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: DonPAPI automates secrets dump remotely on multiple Windows computers, with defense evasion in mind.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: git clone git+https://github.com/login-securite/DonPAPI.git. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `pipx install git+$KFIOSA_TARGET_URL`. Further invocations from upstream: `donpapi collect -u admin -p '$KFIOSA_SECRET' -d domain.local -t ALL --fetch-pvk`; `usage: DonPAPI [-h] [-v] [-o DIRNAME] {collect,gui} ...`. DonPAPI automates secrets dump remotely on multiple Windows computers, with defense evasion in mind.

name: DonPAPI
summary: DonPAPI automates secrets dump remotely on multiple Windows computers, with defense evasion in mind.

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need frameworks, use DonPAPI because DonPAPI automates secrets dump remotely on multiple Windows computers, with defense evasion in mind.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `pipx install git+$KFIOSA_TARGET_URL` as documented upstream for DonPAPI.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `donpapi collect -u admin -p '$KFIOSA_SECRET' -d domain.local -t ALL --fetch-pvk` as documented upstream for DonPAPI.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `usage: DonPAPI [-h] [-v] [-o DIRNAME] {collect,gui} ...` as documented upstream for DonPAPI.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `Dump revelant information on compromised targets without AV detection. Version: 2.0.0` as documented upstream for DonPAPI.
When chaining frameworks tools, pair DonPAPI with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `DonPAPI --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
